creator: jhale@FreeBSD.org
text:
I just have a few concerns.

> -USE_QT_VER=        4
> -QT_COMPONENTS=       moc_build qmake_build rcc_build corelib gui iconengines imageformats network
> +USE_QT4=  moc_build qmake_build rcc_build corelib gui iconengines imageformats network

iconengines and imageformats don't seem to be used by this port, so they can be removed

> +# Pixmaps
> +   ${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/icons/${PORTNAME}.png ${PREFIX}/share/pixmaps/
> +

Is it really neccessary to install an additional icon in share/pixmaps?
The game and .desktop file seem to work fine without it.  If there is a good reason,
I would recommend creating a symlink to share/icons/hicolor/48x48/apps/connectagram.png, e.g.
${LN} -sf ${PREFIX}/share/icons/hicolor/48x48/apps/connectagram.png ${PREFIX}/share/pixmaps/connectagram.png

> -.  for f in ${PORTDOCS}
> -  ${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/${f} ${DOCSDIR}
> -.  endfor
> +  ${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/ChangeLog ${DOCSDIR}

It is better to use what is already defined in PORTDOCS in the install step.
That way if there are changes to PORTDOCS, it only has to be changed in one place.
You can still simplify this to one line using:
${INSTALL_DATA} ${PORTDOCS:S|^|${WRKSRC}/|} ${DOCSDIR}

Also, the pkg-plist should include the data files installed by the port.  The
PORTDATA macro will break the port if the user has NOPORTDATA defined.

- Jason
